title: A virtually 2-step nilpotent group with polynomial geodesic growth
author_string: University of Technology Sydney, Bishop, Alex, Elder, Murray, University of Technology Sydney
abstract: A direct consequence of Gromov's theorem is that if a group has polynomial geodesic growth with respect to some finite generating set then it is virtually nilpotent. However, until now the only examples known were virtually abelian. In this note we furnish an example of a virtually 2-step nilpotent group having polynomial geodesic growth with respect to a certain finite generating set.
